channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: A suspect is in custody after Trump is rushed from correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Trump and several cabinet members were safely rushed from the event in Washington, D.C. after several loud sounds were heard. The Secret Service said one person was in custody.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump and several cabinet members were safely rushed from the event in Washington, D.C. after several loud sounds were heard. The Secret Service said one person was in custody.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: New CEO Steve O'Donnell vows to unite NASCAR and return the fun
item.description: Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its roots.
item.content:encoded:
Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its roots.

(Image credit: Mike McCarn)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: New museum dedicated to AI promises an ethical approach
item.description: As some continue to pushback on AI art, artist Refik Anadol seeks to base his new venture on ethical and environmental principles.
item.content:encoded:
Machine Dreams: Rainforest, the inaugural exhibition at Dataland. The museum dedicated to AI art is scheduled to open on June 20.'/>

As some continue to pushback on AI art, artist Refik Anadol seeks to base his new venture on ethical and environmental principles.

(Image credit: Refik Anadol/Dataland)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Dirk Kempthorne, former Idaho governor and U.S. Interior secretary, dies at 74
item.description: Dirk Kempthorne, a Republican, was elected mayor of Boise at age 34 and served seven years before serving one term in the U.S. Senate and then as governor until 2006.
item.content:encoded:
Dirk Kempthorne, a Republican, was elected mayor of Boise at age 34 and served seven years before serving one term in the U.S. Senate and then as governor until 2006.

(Image credit: Manuel Balce Ceneta)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
item.description: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.
item.content:encoded:
The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

(Image credit: IDREES MOHAMMED)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Rocky Balboa statue takes up a new home inside Philly art museum
item.description: The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.
item.content:encoded:
The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.

(Image credit: Tassanee Vejpongsa)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Roommate charged with two counts of murder in death, disappearance of two USF students
item.description: Authorities have filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.
item.content:encoded:
Authorities have filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.

(Image credit: Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty on same day U.S. approves firing squads
item.description: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.

(Image credit: Christopher Furlong)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Mali hit by wave of coordinated attacks from armed groups
item.description: Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.
item.content:encoded:
Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

(Image credit: uncredited)
item.dc:creator: Jewel Bright

item.title: Opinion: A lesson in humanity at the Boston Marathon
item.description: Two runners in this week's Boston Marathon stopped to help a racer who had collapsed just short of the finish line. NPR's Scott Simon says their generosity is its own kind of "personal best."
item.content:encoded:
Two runners in this week's Boston Marathon stopped to help a racer who had collapsed just short of the finish line. NPR's Scott Simon says their generosity is its own kind of "personal best."

(Image credit: Cj Gunther)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon
